metadata.title: Fly Ash Pricing Trends and Market Dynamics

class_f.description: Low-calcium fly ash from bituminous coal combustion
class_f.trend: Sharply rising due to coal plant retirements reducing fresh supply
class_f.primary_sources: Appalachian coal plants, Midwest coal plants

class_c.description: High-calcium fly ash from sub-bituminous/lignite coal
class_c.trend: Rising but less steeply than Class F; Western coal plants declining more slowly
class_c.primary_sources: Powder River Basin coal plants (WY, MT), Texas lignite plants

regional_pricing.notes: Most coal plants retired; reliance on harvested ash and imports
Major plants still operating in AL, GA, SC
Mix of active and retired plants; harvesting growing
Lignite plants still operating; Class C dominant
PRB coal plants operating; Class C available
Limited local supply; relies on rail from Montana/Wyoming

harvested_ash.description: Reclaimed fly ash from legacy coal ash impoundments and landfills
harvested_ash.quality_considerations: Harvested ash may have variable LOI (loss on ignition); requires beneficiation for ASTM C618 compliance

imported_fly_ash.notes: Growing but limited by quality certification and logistics

cement_replacement_economics.notes: At 25% replacement, saves ~$5/ton concrete and 0.22 tons CO2 per ton concrete
